prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f7d1014e8b62c9","BT":2,"BW":4,"HF":0.53,"RF":0.31,"MA":1,"hour_entropy":2.18,"behavioral_consistency":0.34,"response_latency_variance":7.45,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.52,"error_recovery_pattern":0.072,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.76,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.38,"gas_price_precision":0.59,"rationale":"Seed 1014 personality: a focused-hours, habit-driven human (~−1.2σ hour_entropy, +1.3σ behavioral_consistency) who is sloppy with gas pricing (~−1.1σ gas_price_precision, −0.8σ eip1559_tip_precision) and has erratic timing (+1.5σ response_latency_var), with a narrow action repertoire (−1σ perplexity) and slightly elevated revert rate (+1.3σ error_recovery); all five HasciDB indicators stay safely below thresholds."}
